question: [File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/PublishProperties.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user inject a scenario name containing special characters or Unicode into the `scenarios` map key to bypass the `StringUtils.isBlank()` check in `validate()`, causing a malformed scenario name to propagate into transaction memos and corrupt on-chain memo data?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/PublishProperties.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a `scenarios` map with a whitespace-only key (e.g., a tab character `\\t`) that passes `StringUtils.isBlank()` but is treated as a valid name downstream, causing `property.setName(name)` to set a blank scenario name that gets embedded in every submitted transaction memo?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/PublishProperties.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user set `enabled=true` and provide an empty `scenarios` map to trigger the `IllegalArgumentException` in `validate()`, causing a denial-of-service that prevents all legitimate transactions from being submitted?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/PublishProperties.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getScenarios()] Can an unprivileged external user inject a crafted `PublishScenarioProperties` entry into the `scenarios` LinkedHashMap with a `tps=0.0` value to cause the `CompositeTransactionGenerator.rebuild()` to set `total=0.0`, switching the rate limiter to `INACTIVE_RATE_LIMITER` and halting all transaction submissions?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/PublishProperties.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getBatchDivisor()] Can an unprivileged external user set `batchDivisor` to its minimum allowed value of 100 while configuring an extremely high aggregate `tps` across scenarios, causing `batchSize = ceil(total / batchDivisor)` in `CompositeTransactionGenerator.rebuild()` to produce an oversized batch that floods the network with tampered transactions in a single burst?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/PublishProperties.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getClients()] Can an unprivileged external user set `clients` to `Integer.MAX_VALUE` (bypassing only the `@Min(1)` constraint), causing `getClients()` in `TransactionPublisher` to attempt to instantiate an unbounded number of SDK `Client` objects, exhausting heap memory and preventing any legitimate transactions from being submitted?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/PublishProperties.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getNodeMaxBackoff()] Can an unprivileged external user set `nodeMaxBackoff` to `Duration.ZERO` (which passes the `@NotNull` constraint but has no `@DurationMin`), causing the Hedera SDK client's `setNodeMaxBackoff()` to disable exponential backoff, making the monitor aggressively retry failed nodes and submit duplicate or replayed transactions to the network?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/PublishProperties.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getNodeMaxBackoff()] Can an unprivileged external user set `nodeMaxBackoff` to an extremely large `Duration` value, causing the SDK client to wait indefinitely between node retries, effectively stalling all transaction submissions and creating a denial-of-service against the publish pipeline?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/PublishProperties.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getStatusFrequency()] Can an unprivileged external user set `statusFrequency` to its minimum of 1 second, causing the `@Scheduled` `status()` method in `PublishMetrics` to fire at maximum rate, flooding logs with scenario status and masking tampered transaction error signals in the noise?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/PublishProperties.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getResponseThreads()] Can an unprivileged external user set `responseThreads` to a very large value (no `@Max` constraint), causing the monitor to spawn an unbounded thread pool for processing transaction responses, exhausting OS thread resources and causing legitimate transaction response processing to fail?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/PublishProperties.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getWarmupPeriod()] Can an unprivileged external user set `warmupPeriod` to `Duration.ZERO` (which satisfies `@DurationMin(seconds=0)`), bypassing the warmup ramp-up in `CompositeTransactionGenerator.getRateLimiter()` and causing the full transaction rate to be applied immediately, potentially submitting a burst of tampered transactions before the network is ready?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/PublishProperties.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getWarmupPeriod()] Can an unprivileged external user set `warmupPeriod` to an extremely large `Duration`, causing `RateLimiter.create(tps, warmupPeriod)` in `CompositeTransactionGenerator.getRateLimiter()` to throttle all transaction submissions to near-zero for an extended period, effectively blocking all legitimate transaction publishing?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/PublishProperties.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: isAsync()] Can an unprivileged external user set `async=false` to force `TransactionPublisher.execute()` to use synchronous `executable.execute(client)` instead of `executeAsync()`, blocking the reactive pipeline thread and causing transaction submission throughput to collapse, effectively tampering with the submission rate?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/PublishProperties.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: isEnabled()] Can an unprivileged external user toggle `enabled=false` at runtime to cause `TransactionPublisher.publish()` to return `Mono.empty()` for all requests, silently dropping all transactions without any error signal, making it appear the system is running while no transactions are actually submitted?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/PublishProperties.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user inject a scenario with a name that is exactly 100 characters of whitespace-padded content to pass `StringUtils.isBlank()` while embedding control characters that corrupt the transaction memo when `property.setName(name)` is called and the name is later formatted into `getMemo()`?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/PublishProperties.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getScenarios()] Can an unprivileged external user add a scenario with `type=FREEZE` (a `FreezeTransactionSupplier`) into the `scenarios` map, causing the monitor to submit a network freeze transaction that halts the entire Hedera network, constituting a catastrophic tamper of submitted transactions?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/PublishProperties.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getScenarios()] Can an unprivileged external user configure a scenario with `type=ACCOUNT_DELETE` and a crafted `transferAccountId` in the `properties` map, causing the monitor to submit account deletion transactions that destroy accounts on the network, constituting irreversible tampering of on-chain state?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/PublishProperties.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getScenarios()] Can an unprivileged external user configure a scenario with `type=TOKEN_WIPE` and inject target account IDs via the `properties` map, causing the monitor to submit token wipe transactions that destroy token balances of arbitrary accounts, constituting high-impact tampering of submitted transactions?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/PublishProperties.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getScenarios()] Can an unprivileged external user configure a scenario with `type=TOKEN_BURN` and a large `amount` in the `properties` map, causing the monitor to submit token burn transactions that permanently destroy token supply, constituting irreversible tampering of submitted transactions?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/PublishProperties.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getScenarios()] Can an unprivileged external user configure a scenario with `type=SCHEDULE_DELETE` and inject a target `scheduleId` via the `properties` map, causing the monitor to submit schedule deletion transactions that cancel pending scheduled transactions, constituting tampering of the transaction schedule state?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/PublishProperties.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getScenarios()] Can an unprivileged external user configure a scenario with `type=CONSENSUS_DELETE_TOPIC` and inject a target `topicId` via the `properties` map, causing the monitor to submit topic deletion transactions that permanently destroy HCS topics and all future message submissions to them?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/PublishProperties.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getScenarios()] Can an unprivileged external user configure a scenario with `type=TOKEN_DELETE` and inject a target `tokenId` via the `properties` map, causing the monitor to submit token deletion transactions that permanently destroy token definitions on the network?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/PublishProperties.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getScenarios()] Can an unprivileged external user configure a scenario with `type=CRYPTO_TRANSFER` and set `senderAccountId` to the monitor operator's account and `recipientAccountId` to an attacker-controlled account in the `properties` map, causing the monitor to continuously drain the operator's HBAR balance via submitted transfer transactions?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/PublishProperties.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getScenarios()] Can an unprivileged external user configure a scenario with `type=CRYPTO_TRANSFER`, set `amount` to `Long.MAX_VALUE` in the `properties` map, and trigger an integer overflow in `addCryptoTransfers()` when `Hbar.fromTinybars(amount)` is called, causing a malformed transfer transaction to be submitted?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/PublishProperties.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getScenarios()] Can an unprivileged external user configure a scenario with `type=CONSENSUS_SUBMIT_MESSAGE` and set `messageSize=6144` (the maximum) in the `properties` map, causing the monitor to continuously submit maximum-size messages to an HCS topic, exhausting topic storage and inflating transaction fees?
